topic: A person finds their spouse's secret savings account
raw_output:
[story 1]

The envelope felt impossibly heavy in my trembling hand. It was tucked beneath a stack of old tax documents, something I hadn't noticed until now. My breath hitched, a shallow, quick intake of air that burned in my chest. Inside, a crisp bank statement, clearly belonging to Mark. My eyes darted over the figures—a substantial amount, completely unexplained. A cold knot tightened in my stomach, pulling at my diaphragm. I tried to set the paper down, but my hands were slick with sweat. Every muscle in my body felt rigid, as if I were braced for an impact. Who knew this? The silence of the apartment seemed to amplify the frantic drumming in my ears.

[story 2]

I found the ledger tucked inside a seldom-used desk drawer. The paper was thick, the handwriting precise and unfamiliar. As my fingers traced the entries, a wave of profound unease washed over me. The numbers weren't just large; they were meticulously organized, a hidden world existing just beneath the surface of our shared life. I pulled back sharply, knocking over a nearby lamp. The sudden noise made me flinch violently, my heart hammering against my ribs like a trapped bird. I couldn't look at the page anymore; the implications swam before my eyes, creating a dizzying pressure behind my temples. I needed to leave, needed to get away from this sudden, terrifying revelation.

[story 3]

It was a misplaced folder, sitting innocently on the bottom shelf of the filing cabinet. When I opened it, the contents were a series of deposit slips, dated over the last year. A sudden, sharp constriction seized my throat. My vision tunneled slightly. I kept my gaze fixed on the ink, refusing to acknowledge the reality of what I was seeing. A slow, creeping paralysis began to set in; my limbs felt weighted, uncooperative. I backed away slowly, my heel catching the edge of the cabinet, sending a small clatter echoing through the quiet room. I needed to hide this evidence, needed to bury the knowledge before it could consume me entirely.
